title: Generalising gravitationally induced decoherence beyond linear environmental interactions in a microscopic quantum mechanical toy model
authors: Max Joseph Fahn, Renata Ferrero, Kristina Giesel, Roman Kemper
abstract: We generalise the quantum mechanical toy model for gravitationally induced decoherence presented in Xu, Blencowe (2022) and Domi et al. (2024). In contrast to earlier formulations, in which the Hamiltonian of the system of interest is linearly coupled to the position operators of the oscillators in the environment, we consider an interaction formulated in terms of Weyl elements of the environment's position operators. This extension is motivated by polymer quantum mechanics, in which Weyl elements are fundamental operators, as well as by the possibility of generating non-linear interactions through suitable truncations of the exponential Weyl elements. Here we focus on a sinus-like coupling that is still quantised using the Schrödinger representation and, in the limit of a small Weyl parameter, reproduces the conventional linear interaction. To derive the corresponding master equation, we developed two complementary methods for the analytical calculation of the environmental correlation functions. The first utilises Wick's theorem for thermal expectation values in conjunction with annihilation and creation operators, while the second is based on the short-time Fourier transform and completely avoids the use of annihilation and creation operators, making it more readily transferable and generalisable to a polymer quantisation. Both approaches yield identical results. We further generalise the spectral density required for the exponential coupling structure. A numerical analysis shows that the environmental correlation functions decay rapidly with time, which supports the validity of the Markov approximation. Using a Taylor expansion in the Weyl parameter, we show that the first-order term reproduces the decoherence model of Xu, Blencowe (2022) and Domi et al. (2024). Finally, we derive the solution to the renormalised master equation.
summary: This paper extends existing models of gravitationally induced decoherence by replacing linear position coupling with non-linear Weyl element coupling. It introduces a new method for calculating environmental correlations that is compatible with polymer quantum mechanics and shows that the model reduces to standard decoherence models in the limit of small Weyl parameters.
main_problem: Generalizing models of gravitationally induced decoherence to include non-linear environmental interactions, specifically moving beyond linear coupling to position operators to accommodate polymer quantum mechanics.
main_result: The authors derived a renormalized master equation for a Weyl-element coupling model and demonstrated that it recovers standard linear decoherence models in the small-parameter limit while providing a framework for polymer quantization.
method: The study employs two analytical approaches: using Wick's theorem with annihilation/creation operators and a Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) method, alongside Taylor expansions and renormalization techniques.
model_or_system: A microscopic quantum mechanical toy model where a system of interest is coupled to a thermal bath of harmonic oscillators via non-linear Weyl elements (sine-like coupling).
key_observables: Environmental correlation functions (one-point and two-point), density matrix elements, and the decoherence/dissipation rate.
important_parameters: Weyl parameter (mu_0), coupling strength (g), inverse temperature (beta), and high-frequency cutoff (Omega).
main_assumptions: The environment is a bath of harmonic oscillators, the system Hamiltonian is time-independent with a discrete spectrum, and the master equation is truncated at the second order in the coupling parameter.
figures_summary: Figure 3 shows the time evolution of the real part of the density matrix elements, comparing unitary evolution, the zeroth-order linear model, and the higher-order exponential coupling model.
paper_structure: The paper defines the non-linear interaction Hamiltonian, develops dual calculation methods (Wick's theorem and STFT) for correlation functions, generalizes the spectral density, validates the Markov approximation, and concludes with the derivation and solution of the renormalized master equation.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant to researchers in open quantum systems as it provides new mathematical tools (STFT) for calculating correlation functions in non-linear regimes and bridges the gap between standard Schrödinger quantization and Loop Quantum Gravity-inspired polymer quantization.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Numerical evaluation of the real and imaginary part with parameters T = 0.9K, η = 10−8s, g = 0.1, Ω= 1Hz. These are obtained by taking the real/imaginary part of (3.55) after having numerically integrated over ω. The dashed line is the behaviour at vanishing parameter µ0, while the continuous line is the full numerical result at µ0 = 10.

FIG. 2. Numerical evaluation of the real and imaginary decoherence factors with parameters T = 0.9K, ˜η = 10−8√s, ˜g = 0.1√s, Ω= 1Hz for varying values of the parameter µ0. The sums in (3.79) and (3.83) has been truncated at kmax = 100. The dashed lines represent the analytic series expansion in (3.63) (left) and (3.65) (right), while the continuous lines correspond to the numerical results. We observe that the series expansion is in very good agreement for small values of the parameter µ0.
FIG. 3. The time evolution of Re[ρij(t)] given in (4.10) is plotted for varying time t −t0 and a constant energy difference Ej−Ek = 10−9 eV, ˜g = 0.1√s, ˜η = 2·10−17√s, µ0 = 1 10 πβℏ
